decision: Run deferred real-KVM e2e for issue #227 AC1 in a disposable vagrant/libvirt VM (nested KVM). Host agent lacks kvm group + passwordless sudo, so FC cannot run on host.
approach: Prefer lighter runtime-level check: firecracker.Manager Start->Pause->CreateSnapshot->Kill->LoadSnapshot with Network.Mode=none, assert FC GET / state=Running on fresh process. Install firecracker v1.15 in VM (host is v1.7).

detail: Runtime-level AC1 check on real nested KVM (libvirt VM, firecracker v1.15.0, branch tip 2e74764). Start->Running, Pause->Paused, CreateSnapshot (snap 13669B + mem 512MB), Kill pid14170, LoadSnapshot fresh pid14182, GET / -> Running. resume_vm:true schema confirmed in FC log; guest continued executing post-resume (console 32529->34210). marker survival PASS.

decision: Did NOT run full daemon-level flow.
rationale: Its only KVM-gated call is the identical runtimeManager.LoadSnapshot already validated with full FC-log evidence. Remaining surface (state guards, snapshot ownership resolution, locking, tap re-establishment, DB transitions) is control-plane logic covered by internal/api/snapshot_handlers_test.go. The exec-marker step needs SSH-key-into-guest + NAT/iptables setup unrelated to issue #227 code; a networking failure there would add no AC1 signal.

decision: Re-run real-KVM e2e against ACTUAL PR head 42b3b10 (origin/feat/issue-227-snapshot-load, PR #268). Prior run tested stale 2e74764 (resume_vm:true). Current code: PUT /snapshot/load resume_vm:false, then separate PATCH /vm {Resumed}. Verified at host git show 42b3b10:internal/firecracker/vm.go lines 757 (ResumeVM:false) and 731 (PATCH Resumed).

PASS. Real-KVM e2e re-run on ACTUAL PR head 42b3b10 (verified git rev-parse HEAD=42b3b10975854000949ef1081c68ee7ed672df2c inside vagrant libvirt VM, nested KVM, FC v1.15.0, go1.25.4). go build ./... OK. Harness: boot Running -> Pause -> CreateSnapshot(snap 13669B, mem 512MiB) -> Kill(pid14174) -> LoadSnapshot(fresh pid14186) -> GET / = Running; marker survival PASS (console grew 32529->34666 post-resume). CRITICAL PROOF from Firecracker stderr (console.log) on fresh load process: PUT /snapshot/load body includes resume_vm:false, FOLLOWED BY PATCH /vm {state:Resumed}, then 'resume vm' VMM action took 47us. Confirms current load-paused-then-resume code path (NOT stale resume_vm:true). AC1 validated on current PR head.

result: AC1 core claim CONFIRMED: guest state (memory+disk) survives kill+resume-from-snapshot via a brand-new Firecracker process. Verified with a marker file written pre-snapshot, read post-resume over SSH, on two independent VMs (snaptest, snap3).
finding: REPRODUCIBLE REGRESSION (100% repro, 2/2 runs): after resume-from-snapshot, guest systemd hangs ~90s on dev-ttyS0.device, times out, serial-getty@ttyS0.service fails (Dependency failed). Serial console is broken post-resume; SSH/network unaffected. Root cause not yet isolated - suspect guest udev coldplug/uevent not redelivered for the serial UART across the fresh-process boundary (a known Firecracker snapshot/restore + systemd device-unit interaction).
secondary_findings: nanofuse vm snapshot list <vm> returns an empty table immediately after a successful snapshot create (list/create inconsistency)
Error message on 'snapshot create' while running says 'must be in running or stopped state' but state was already running - the real requirement is 'paused', message is misleading
VM state DB still showed 'paused' after the backing Firecracker process was killed out-of-band (pkill) - no reconciliation/health check catches a dead process
disposition: escalated to operator before deciding fix scope (touches images/base guest image = infrastructure change per repo policy)

correction: Prior hypothesis WRONG. The ttyS0 hang was attributed to snapshot-resume and to upstream Firecracker PR #5764. A discriminating test disproves both: a FRESH, never-snapshotted microVM (FC 1.16.1) hits the IDENTICAL 'Timed out waiting for device dev-ttyS0.device' + serial-getty Dependency-failed at ~90s. The hang occurs on EVERY boot of images/base, independent of snapshot/resume and of the Firecracker version (same at 1.7.0 and 1.16.1).
impact_on_227: Issue #227 AC1 is VALIDATED on real KVM regardless: pre-snapshot guest marker (written over SSH) survives graceful vm-stop (old FC process terminated) + resume-from-snapshot into a fresh FC process (PID confirmed live, GET / => state:Running). Guest memory+disk state genuinely restores. The ttyS0 hang was a red herring, never a #227 regression.
impact_on_bump: The FC 1.7.0->1.16.1 bump does NOT fix the ttyS0 hang. It stands on its own merits only (no-stale-artifacts policy; fixes unrelated upstream vsock/UART-TX post-restore bugs). Commit message on fix/vagrant-firecracker-stale-pin corrected to say so. Operator informed the original stated justification was wrong; keep-or-drop is operator's call.
followup: ttyS0 hang filed separately as an images/base defect; out of scope for the 4-branch/3-issue goal; blocks nothing (multi-user.target reached, SSH works).
